ELICOS_INFO.title: Strategic Advice for ELICOS Programs
description: When a student's English scores do not meet the requirements for their main course (like a diploma or degree), ELICOS is the perfect pathway. However, the **length** of the ELICOS program is a critical factor for visa success, especially for Filipino applicants.

list: **Advised Program Length:** For most students, a **10-week ELICOS course is the safest and most recommended duration**. This shows a clear intent to quickly meet the English requirement and move on to the main course of study.

**Alternative for Longer Programs:** If the school advises a 20-week program, it is better to **retake the English test** (IELTS, PTE, etc.). Passing the test directly is the strongest possible evidence of English proficiency.

description: Please advise the student that while a 20-week program is an option, it carries a **higher risk of visa refusal**.

WORK_RIGHTS_VOCATIONAL_SINGLE.title: VET Student Work Rights (Subclass 500)
description: For students studying Vocational Education and Training (Certificates, Diplomas, or Trade Qualifications).

list: **Definition of a Fortnight:** A 14-day period starting on a Monday.
**While Course is in Session:** The student can work a maximum of 48 hours per fortnight.
**Practical Work:** If the course requires "Work Placement" (e.g., a Chef or Childcare student), those specific hours do not count toward the 48-hour limit, as they are a mandatory part of the study.
**Unlimited Work:** The student can work unlimited hours during official school breaks.
**National Minimum Wage:** $24.95 AUD per hour (current rate).
**No Working Early:** The student cannot start working until their first day of class.
**TFN Required:** The student needs a Tax File Number (TFN) to be paid legally and avoid being taxed at the highest rate.

WORK_RIGHTS_VOCATIONAL_DEPENDENT.title: VET Student Work Rights (Subclass 500)
description: For students studying Vocational Education and Training (Certificates, Diplomas, or Advanced Diplomas).

list: **Definition of a Fortnight:** A 14-day period starting on a Monday.
**While Course is in Session:** The student can work a maximum of 48 hours per fortnight.
**Practical Work:** If the course requires "Work Placement" (e.g., a Chef or Childcare student), those specific hours do not count toward the 48-hour limit, as they are a mandatory part of the study.
**Unlimited Work:** The student can work unlimited hours during official school breaks.

description: For spouses or partners of students enrolled in Certificates, Diplomas, or Advanced Diplomas.

list: **Standard Rule:** Dependents of VET students are limited to 48 hours per fortnight at all times.

description: **No "Full-Time" Holidays:** Unlike the primary student (who can work unlimited hours during school breaks), the dependent is always capped at 48 hours per fortnight, even during the student's summer holidays.

list: **National Minimum Wage:** $24.95 AUD per hour (current rate).
**No Working Early:** The student cannot start working until their first day of class.
**TFN Required:** The student needs a Tax File Number (TFN) to be paid legally and avoid being taxed at the highest rate.

ELICOS_WORK_RIGHTS_SINGLE.title: ELICOS Student Work Rights (Subclass 500)
description: For students enrolled in full-time English Language Intensive Courses.

list: **While Course is in Session:** The student can work a maximum of 48 hours per fortnight.
**Intensive Study:** Since ELICOS requires at least 20 hours of face-to-face classes per week, the student must carefully balance their 24 hours of work (average) with their studies.
**Calculation:** This is a rolling 14-day period starting on a Monday.
**Unlimited Work:** The student can work unlimited hours during official course breaks.

**Short Breaks:** ELICOS courses are often shorter (e.g., 10, 20, or 30 weeks). The student only gets unlimited work rights during the breaks between their modules or if they have a scheduled vacation period approved by the school.
**National Minimum Wage:** $24.95 AUD per hour (current rate).
**No Working Early:** The student cannot start working until their first day of English class.
**TFN Required:** The student must apply for a Tax File Number (TFN) to ensure they are taxed correctly.

ELICOS_WORK_RIGHTS_DEPENDENT.title: ELICOS Student Work Rights (Subclass 500)
description: For students enrolled in full-time English Language Intensive Courses.

list: **While Course is in Session:** The student can work a maximum of 48 hours per fortnight.
**Intensive Study:** Since ELICOS requires at least 20 hours of face-to-face classes per week, the student must carefully balance their 24 hours of work (average) with their studies.
**Calculation:** This is a rolling 14-day period starting on a Monday.
**Unlimited Work:** The student can work unlimited hours during official course breaks.

**Short Breaks:** ELICOS courses are often shorter (e.g., 10, 20, or 30 weeks). The student only gets unlimited work rights during the breaks between their modules or if they have a scheduled vacation period approved by the school.
**Limited Hours:** If the student brings a partner, they are also capped at 48 hours per fortnight at all times. They do not get unlimited work rights, even when the student is on a school break.
**National Minimum Wage:** $24.95 AUD per hour (current rate).
**No Working Early:** The student cannot start working until their first day of English class.
**TFN Required:** The student must apply for a Tax File Number (TFN) to ensure they are taxed correctly.
